title: The Duke's Son Claims He Won't Love Me Yet Showers Me with Adoration Anime Reveals More Cast
content: A stage presentation at AnimeJapan 2026 on Sunday for the television anime for writer Kei Misawa and artist Natsu Mizuno's The Duke's Son Claims He Won't Love Me Yet Showers Me with Adoration ("Kimi o Koi Suru Ki wa Nai" to Itta Jiki Kōshaku-sama ga Nazeka Dekiai Shitekimasu) manga revealed two new cast members on Sunday, as well as character visuals for Elsa Yukarainen and Julius Roias.The new cast includes:Kaito Ishikawa as Yalmo Parnila Chika Anzai as Serafina Parnila Yui Ishikawa stars as Elsa Yukarainen, and Sōma Saitō voices Julius Roias.Image via Kimi wo Koisuru Ki wa Nai's X/Twitter© 水埜なつ・三沢ケイ・フレックスコミックス／きみ愛製作委員会The anime will premiere in July on TV Asahi and BS Asahi.Hitoyuki Matsui (Dokkoida?!, Puppet Master Sakon) is directing the anime at Zero-G and Grass with Kaoru Suzuki credited for planning technical direction. Tomoko Konparu (Blue Spring Ride, Dance with Devils) is overseeing the series scripts, and Yūko Yahiro (Aharen-san wa Hakarenai, Attack on Titan: Junior High) is designing the characters. MICHIRU (Ascendance of a Bookworm, Given) is composing the music. Saber Links is working on sound production.Flex Comix describes the manga's story:Elsa, the daughter of a fallen aristocrat, receives news.It was a marriage proposal from Julius, a very elite nobleman.Elsa is wondering,” Why would such a distinguished man marry me"?But after the wedding, Julius, who had been kind and gentle up to that point, changed completely!In a cold voice, he tells her, ”I have no intention of loving you.”Misawa and Mizuno debuted the series on the BookLive digital bookstore in February 2021. Flex Comix shipped the fifth volume on March 13. Flex Comix also offers the manga in English on various digital platforms.Source: The Duke's Son Claims He Won't Love Me Yet Showers Me with Adoration anime's website
author: ANN

contentBlocks.text: A stage presentation at AnimeJapan 2026 on Sunday for the television anime for writer Kei Misawa and artist Natsu Mizuno 's The Duke's Son Claims He Won't Love Me Yet Showers Me with Adoration ( "Kimi o Koi Suru Ki wa Nai" to Itta Jiki Kōshaku-sama ga Nazeka Dekiai Shitekimasu ) manga revealed two new cast members on Sunday, as well as character visuals for Elsa Yukarainen and Julius Roias.
The new cast includes:
Yui Ishikawa stars as Elsa Yukarainen, and Sōma Saitō voices Julius Roias.

contentBlocks.credit: Image via Kimi wo Koisuru Ki wa Nai's X/Twitter

contentBlocks.text: Hitoyuki Matsui ( Dokkoida?! , Puppet Master Sakon ) is directing the anime at Zero-G and Grass with Kaoru Suzuki credited for planning technical direction. Tomoko Konparu ( Blue Spring Ride , Dance with Devils ) is overseeing the series scripts, and Yūko Yahiro ( Aharen-san wa Hakarenai , Attack on Titan: Junior High ) is designing the characters. MICHIRU ( Ascendance of a Bookworm , Given ) is composing the music. Saber Links is working on sound production.
Flex Comix describes the manga's story:

Elsa, the daughter of a fallen aristocrat, receives news. It was a marriage proposal from Julius, a very elite nobleman. Elsa is wondering,” Why would such a distinguished man marry me"? But after the wedding, Julius, who had been kind and gentle up to that point, changed completely! In a cold voice, he tells her, ”I have no intention of loving you.”

Misawa and Mizuno debuted the series on the BookLive digital bookstore in February 2021. Flex Comix shipped the fifth volume on March 13. Flex Comix also offers the manga in English on various digital platforms.

title: 20th Anniversary Sgt. Frog Film Unveils June 26 Opening, 2 New Characters
content: The AnimeJapan 2026 stage panel for the Sgt. Frog franchise announced that Shin Gekijōban ☆ Keroro Gunsō Fukkatsu Shite Sokkō Chikyū Metsubō no Kiki de Arimasu! (New Sgt. Frog the Movie: The Earth Is in Mortal Peril as Soon as We Come Back!), the new 20th anniversary anime film project, will open in Japan on June 26. The panel also unveiled two new characters that the film is introducing: Aruru Image courtesy of Cannonball© 吉崎観音／ KADOKAWA ・劇場版 ケロロ 軍曹製作委員会Deruru Image courtesy of Cannonball© 吉崎観音／ KADOKAWA ・劇場版 ケロロ 軍曹製作委員会 Update: The two new characters are brothers from the planet Keron who apparently have a past link to Sgt Frog's unit. In addition, selected episodes of the earlier Sgt. Frog television anime will begin broadcasting again in Japan on April 6 at 3:20 a.m. Image via Gekijōban ☆ Keroro Gunsō Fukkatsu Shite Sokkō Chikyū Metsubō no Kiki de Arimasu! film's website©吉崎観音／KADOKAWA・劇場版ケロロ軍曹製作委員会 Yūichi Fukuda (live-action Gintama, Saint Young Men, HK/Hentai Kamen) is the supervising director and scriptwriter for the film, with Fumitoshi Oizaki (Sgt. Frog character designer, Romeo X Juliet, Etotama, Akuma-kun, The Gorilla God's Go-To Girl) directing the anime at Bandai Namco Pictures, and Satoshi Koike (Etotama, Soul Eater Not!, Princess Connect! Re:Dive season 2) as character designer.In addition to the film, an all-new Sgt. Frog television anime, titled Keroro Gunsō ☆, will debut in fall 2026. The television anime will have a brand-new cast, while this summer's film will still feature the existing cast in the franchise. Crunchyroll added the earlier television anime to its catalog beginning in 2021, and it describes the anime:Keroro is Sergeant Frog - the leader of a platoon of warriors sent to conquer planet Earth. But when the amphibious invaders discovered how much humans enjoy dissecting their comrades, the troops were scattered, the mission was aborted, and Keroro was abandoned. Now, this once proud soldier spends more time wielding a vacuum than he does the weapons of war. It's a far cry from the glory of battle, but hey, at least he's got his own room. And though the invasion may have slowed to a hop, Sergeant Frog still occasionally yearns to overcome his karaoke addiction and reassemble his troops.Sunrise's earlier anime series based on Mine Yoshizaki's Sgt. Frog (Keroro Gunsō) manga ran from 2004 to 2011, and it spawned five feature films. Funimation released two "seasons" of the anime on DVD, after ADV Films had licensed but not released the anime.Discotek licensed the anime, and its release of Sgt. Frog includes all 358 episodes in successive SD Blu-ray Disc volume releases by season, beginning in 2021. Discotek released seasons 5-7 last year.American manga publisher Tokyopop released twenty volumes of the original manga in North America before shutting its doors in 2011.Source: AnimeJapan 2026Disclosure: Kadokawa World Entertainment (KWE), a wholly owned subsidiary of Kadokawa Corporation, is the majority owner of Anime News Network, LLC. One or more of the companies mentioned in this article are part of the Kadokawa Group of Companies.
author: ANN

contentBlocks.text: The AnimeJapan 2026 stage panel for the Sgt. Frog franchise announced that Shin Gekijōban ☆ Keroro Gunsō Fukkatsu Shite Sokkō Chikyū Metsubō no Kiki de Arimasu! (New Sgt. Frog the Movie: The Earth Is in Mortal Peril as Soon as We Come Back!), the new 20th anniversary anime film project , will open in Japan on June 26. The panel also unveiled two new characters that the film is introducing:
Deruru
Update: The two new characters are brothers from the planet Keron who apparently have a past link to Sgt Frog's unit.
In addition, selected episodes of the earlier Sgt. Frog television anime will begin broadcasting again in Japan on April 6 at 3:20 a.m.

contentBlocks.credit: Image via Gekijōban ☆ Keroro Gunsō Fukkatsu Shite Sokkō Chikyū Metsubō no Kiki de Arimasu! film's website

contentBlocks.text: In addition to the film, an all-new Sgt. Frog television anime , titled Keroro Gunsō ☆ , will debut in fall 2026. The television anime will have a brand-new cast, while this summer's film will still feature the existing cast in the franchise .
Crunchyroll added the earlier television anime to its catalog beginning in 2021, and it describes the anime:

Keroro is Sergeant Frog - the leader of a platoon of warriors sent to conquer planet Earth. But when the amphibious invaders discovered how much humans enjoy dissecting their comrades, the troops were scattered, the mission was aborted, and Keroro was abandoned. Now, this once proud soldier spends more time wielding a vacuum than he does the weapons of war. It's a far cry from the glory of battle, but hey, at least he's got his own room. And though the invasion may have slowed to a hop, Sergeant Frog still occasionally yearns to overcome his karaoke addiction and reassemble his troops.

Sunrise's earlier anime series based on Mine Yoshizaki 's Sgt. Frog ( Keroro Gunsō ) manga ran from 2004 to 2011, and it spawned five feature films. Funimation released two "seasons" of the anime on DVD, after ADV Films had licensed but not released the anime.

Discotek licensed the anime, and its release of Sgt. Frog includes all 358 episodes in successive SD Blu-ray Disc volume releases by season, beginning in 2021. Discotek released seasons 5-7 last year.
American manga publisher Tokyopop released twenty volumes of the original manga in North America before shutting its doors in 2011.
this article has been modified since it was originally posted; see change history

title: 'Tokyo Revengers: Santen Sensou-hen' Reveals Additional Cast, Second Promo for Fall 2026
content:
The Pony Canyon booth at AnimeJapan 2026 revealed four additional cast and a second promotional video for Tokyo Revengers: Santen Sensou-hen ( Tokyo Revengers: Three Deities arc ) television anime on Sunday. The anime is scheduled to premiere in October 2026.

Cast Takeomi Akashi: Naoyuki Shimozuru ( Binan Koukou Chikyuu Bouei-bu Happy Kiss! ) Keizo Arashi: Takanori Hoshino ( Mob Psycho 100 II ) Wakasa Imaushi: Shinnosuke Tachibana ( Tokyo Ghoul ) Minami Terano: Hiroki Yasumoto ( One Punch Man )

Maki Kodaira ( Nanatsu no Taizai: Mokushiroku no Yonkishi ) is directing the new season at LIDENFILMS . Yasuyuki Mutou ( Deadman Wonderland ) is handling the series composition. Kenichi Oonuki ( Golden Kamuy ), Keiko Oota ( Gyakuten Saiban: Sono "Shinjitsu", Igi Ari! ), and Airi Tsuyuki ( Ookami to Koushinryou: Merchant Meets the Wise Wolf chief animation director) are designing the characters. Hiroaki Tsutsumi ( Dr. Stone ) is composing the music.

The anime adapts Ken Wakui 's delinquent action manga , which ran in Weekly Shounen Magazine from March 2017 to November 2022. Kodansha published the 31st and final volume in January 2023.

Kodansha USA began releasing the manga digitally in English in 2018. The 31th and final volume went on sale in June 2023. Seven Seas Entertainment licensed the manga in English for physical release in November 2021. The 15th and final omnibus volume is scheduled to be released on March 25.

PV 2
author: MyAnimeList

PV 2

title: 'Tougen Anki: Nikko Kegon no Taki-hen' Announces Additional Cast Pair for Fall 2026
content:
The Tougen Anki: Nikko Kegon no Taki-hen ( Tougen Anki: Nikko Kegon Falls Arc ) AnimeJapan 2026 stage event at the Pony Canyon booth announced an additional cast pair on Sunday. The second season adapting Yura Urushibara 's action fantasy manga will air on Nippon Television beginning in October 2026.

Voice actors Nobuhiko Okamoto ( Kimetsu no Yaiba ) and Kent Itou ( [Oshi no Ko] ) are joining the cast as Hagure Nekosaki and Yuu Innami, respectively.

Ato Nonaka ( Fate/Zero episode director) is returning to direct at Studio Hibari . Yukie Sugawara ( Overlord ) is handling series composition and script. Ryouko Amisaki ( Radiant 2nd Season ) is designing the characters and Kohta Yamamoto ( Mato Seihei no Slave ) is composing the music.

Produced by Studio Hibari, the first season aired in 24 episodes from Summer 2025 to Fall 2025. Crunchyroll and Netflix simulcasted the anime with subtitles in multiple languages.

Urushibara began serializing the manga in Weekly Shounen Champion in June 2020. Akita Shoten published the 28th volume on February 6, with the 29th volume scheduled to be released on Apri 8. Tougen Anki has a cumulative 5.5 million copies of its volumes in circulation.

Yen Press licensed the manga in English in March 2024 and published the fourth volume in November 2025, with the fifth volume planned for a May 26 release.
author: MyAnimeList

title: Tune in to the Midnight Heart Anime's 2nd Season Video Unveils New Visual
content: NBC Universal unveiled a new video and visual for the second season of hte television anime of Masakuni Igarashi's Tune In to the Midnight Heart (Mayonaka Heart Tune) manga during a panel for the anime on Sunday. Image courtesy of NBC Universal© 五十嵐正邦・講談社 Image via Tune Into the Midnight Heart anime's website©五十嵐正邦・講談社／「真夜中ハートチューン」製作委員会 The first season premiered on January 6, and aired on Kansai TV and Fuji TV's newly announced "Ka-Anival!!" programming block that airs on Tuesdays at 11:00 p.m. JST. The anime also aired on BS Asahi starting on January 9. The anime's 12th and final episode aired on March 24. Crunchyroll streamed the anime, and it also streamed an English dub. Masayuki Takahashi (Butareba: The Story of a Man Turned into a Pig) directed the anime at Gekkō, and Yukie Sugawara (Overlord) was in charge of series scripts. Tomoyuki Shitaya (Food Wars! Shokugeki no Soma, Bakuman.) designed the characters. Suisei Hoshimachi performs the opening theme song "Tsuki ni Mukatte Ute" (Attack Toward the Moon), and Soala performs the ending theme song "Koe no Kiseki" (Trails of Voices). Kodansha's K MANGA service is publishing the manga digitally in English, and it describes the story: When Arisu Yamabuki was all alone in bed at night, he was able to find solace in the voice of a radio host who went by “Apollo.” However, one day, she simply stopped broadcasting without any explanation. Years then passed, and Arisu is now a second-year high-schooler. He makes it his mission to search for Apollo, as there is something he wants to tell her. He doesn't know what she looks like, or even what her real name is, but he manages to get some leads on her in his school's broadcasting club. That's where he meets four girls who all dream to get a job where they can make full use of their voices! Just who is Apollo, and how will those four's dreams pan out? Igarashi (Senryū Girl, Detectives These Days Are Crazy!) launched the manga in Kodansha's Weekly Shōnen Magazine in September 2023. The 12th compiled book volume shipped on March 17. Kodansha USA Publishing licensed the manga and released the sixth volume on March 10.Source: Press release
author: ANN

contentBlocks.text: NBC Universal unveiled a new video and visual for the second season of hte television anime of Masakuni Igarashi 's Tune In to the Midnight Heart ( Mayonaka Heart Tune ) manga during a panel for the anime on Sunday.

contentBlocks.credit: Image via Tune Into the Midnight Heart anime's website

contentBlocks.text: The first season premiered on January 6, and aired on Kansai TV and Fuji TV 's newly announced "Ka-Anival!!" programming block that airs on Tuesdays at 11:00 p.m. JST. The anime also aired on BS Asahi starting on January 9. The anime's 12th and final episode aired on March 24. Crunchyroll streamed the anime, and it also streamed an English dub .

Masayuki Takahashi ( Butareba: The Story of a Man Turned into a Pig ) directed the anime at Gekkō , and Yukie Sugawara (Overlord) was in charge of series scripts. Tomoyuki Shitaya ( Food Wars! Shokugeki no Soma , Bakuman. ) designed the characters.
Suisei Hoshimachi performs the opening theme song "Tsuki ni Mukatte Ute" (Attack Toward the Moon), and Soala performs the ending theme song "Koe no Kiseki" (Trails of Voices).
Kodansha 's K MANGA service is publishing the manga digitally in English, and it describes the story:

When Arisu Yamabuki was all alone in bed at night, he was able to find solace in the voice of a radio host who went by “Apollo.” However, one day, she simply stopped broadcasting without any explanation. Years then passed, and Arisu is now a second-year high-schooler. He makes it his mission to search for Apollo, as there is something he wants to tell her. He doesn't know what she looks like, or even what her real name is, but he manages to get some leads on her in his school's broadcasting club. That's where he meets four girls who all dream to get a job where they can make full use of their voices! Just who is Apollo, and how will those four's dreams pan out?

Igarashi ( Senryū Girl , Detectives These Days Are Crazy! ) launched the manga in Kodansha 's Weekly Shōnen Magazine in September 2023. The 12th compiled book volume shipped on March 17. Kodansha USA Publishing licensed the manga and released the sixth volume on March 10.

title: Recommendation from Mr. Iwamoto Anime's 2nd Promo Video Unveils More Cast Members, July debut
content: Hakuhodo's stage presentation at the AnimeJapan 2026 event on Sunday revealed a new visual, additional cast members, the second promotional video, and the July premiere for the television anime of Hiroshi Shiibashi's Iwamoto-senpai no Suisen (Recommendation from Mr. Iwamoto) manga. Image courtesy of Hakuhodo DY Music & Pictures©椎橋寛/集英社・「岩元先輩ノ推薦」製作委員会The newly announced cast are:Akira Ishida as Bōkyo KizujōImage courtesy of Hakuhodo DY Music & Pictures©椎橋寛/集英社・「岩元先輩ノ推薦」製作委員会Masaya Fukunishi as Oni OkuakiImage courtesy of Hakuhodo DY Music & Pictures©椎橋寛/集英社・「岩元先輩ノ推薦」製作委員会Takuma Nagatsuka as Shizuma AonumaImage courtesy of Hakuhodo DY Music & Pictures©椎橋寛/集英社・「岩元先輩ノ推薦」製作委員会Image courtesy of Hakuhodo DY Music & Pictures Inc©椎橋寛/集英社・「岩元先輩ノ推薦」製作委員会The anime will star:Taito Ban as Kodō IwamotoYuki Sakakihara as Kai HaramachiKent Itō as Sōichirō AmahaToshifumi Kawase (Tenjho Tenge, Shion no Oh, Yakuza Fiancé: Raise wa Tanin ga Ii) is directing the anime at Studio DEEN. Keiichirō Ōchi (The Quintessential Quintuplets; Girlfriend, Girlfriend) is overseeing the series scripts, and Atsuko Nakajima (Hakuoki: Demon of the Fleeting Blossom, Komi Can't Communicate) is the character designer. Takuya Hiramitsu (The Prince of Tennis, Yu-Gi-Oh! 5D's) is the sound director, and Yoshiaki Dewa (Hell's Paradise, Call of the Night) and Tōru Ishitsuka (Gekijōban Ansatsu Kyōshitsu Minna no Jikan, Fate/Grand Order: First Order) are composing the music.The manga takes place in the 1910s, and centers on Kodō Iwamoto, a third-year student at Seihō Middle School, which is directly controlled by the military and is dedicated to the collection and research of supernatural phenomena to harness that power for military use. Under orders from the military, Kodō travels around the country to investigate supernatural phenomena. In one town where it is rumored that "black snow" falls, he meets a boy who thinks his mysterious powers are an illness. Kodō shows the boy how to use his powers, and offers him a letter of recommendation to Seihō Middle School. As he travels to other areas, Kodō encounters many other mysterious events.Shiibashi launched the manga in Shueisha's Ultra Jump magazine in February 2021, after publishing a one-shot pilot in Shueisha's Shonen Jump+ app in March 2020. Shueisha published the 13th compiled volume on January 19.Shiibashi's Nura: Rise of the Yokai Clan (Nurarihyon no Mago) manga ran from 2008 to 2012 with 25 volumes and inspired two television anime seasons in 2010 and 2011. Viz Media published the manga in English. Shiibashi published four more chapters for the manga in 2023.Shiibashi is the creator of the Illegal Rare, Tsukumogami: Teito Jōhōbu Tokkenchobōin Serosero Nanashiki, and Yui Kamio Lets Loose manga.Sources: AnimeJapan 2026 stage presentation, Press release
author: ANN

contentBlocks.text: Hakuhodo's stage presentation at the AnimeJapan 2026 event on Sunday revealed a new visual, additional cast members, the second promotional video, and the July premiere for the television anime of Hiroshi Shiibashi 's Iwamoto-senpai no Suisen ( Recommendation from Mr. Iwamoto ) manga.

contentBlocks.credit: Image courtesy of Hakuhodo DY Music & Pictures

contentBlocks.text: The newly announced cast are:
Akira Ishida as Bōkyo Kizujō

contentBlocks.credit: Image courtesy of Hakuhodo DY Music & Pictures

contentBlocks.text: Masaya Fukunishi as Oni Okuaki

contentBlocks.credit: Image courtesy of Hakuhodo DY Music & Pictures

contentBlocks.text: Takuma Nagatsuka as Shizuma Aonuma

contentBlocks.credit: Image courtesy of Hakuhodo DY Music & Pictures
Image courtesy of Hakuhodo DY Music & Pictures Inc

contentBlocks.text: The anime will star:
- Taito Ban as Kodō Iwamoto
- Yuki Sakakihara as Kai Haramachi
- Kent Itō as Sōichirō Amaha

Toshifumi Kawase ( Tenjho Tenge , Shion no Oh , Yakuza Fiancé: Raise wa Tanin ga Ii ) is directing the anime at Studio DEEN . Keiichirō Ōchi ( The Quintessential Quintuplets ; Girlfriend, Girlfriend ) is overseeing the series scripts, and Atsuko Nakajima ( Hakuoki: Demon of the Fleeting Blossom , Komi Can't Communicate ) is the character designer. Takuya Hiramitsu ( The Prince of Tennis , Yu-Gi-Oh! 5D's ) is the sound director, and Yoshiaki Dewa ( Hell's Paradise , Call of the Night ) and Tōru Ishitsuka ( Gekijōban Ansatsu Kyōshitsu Minna no Jikan , Fate/Grand Order: First Order ) are composing the music.

The manga takes place in the 1910s, and centers on Kodō Iwamoto, a third-year student at Seihō Middle School, which is directly controlled by the military and is dedicated to the collection and research of supernatural phenomena to harness that power for military use. Under orders from the military, Kodō travels around the country to investigate supernatural phenomena. In one town where it is rumored that "black snow" falls, he meets a boy who thinks his mysterious powers are an illness. Kodō shows the boy how to use his powers, and offers him a letter of recommendation to Seihō Middle School. As he travels to other areas, Kodō encounters many other mysterious events.

Shiibashi launched the manga in Shueisha 's Ultra Jump magazine in February 2021, after publishing a one-shot pilot in Shueisha 's Shonen Jump+ app in March 2020. Shueisha published the 13th compiled volume on January 19.

Shiibashi's Nura: Rise of the Yokai Clan ( Nurarihyon no Mago ) manga ran from 2008 to 2012 with 25 volumes and inspired two television anime seasons in 2010 and 2011. Viz Media published the manga in English. Shiibashi published four more chapters for the manga in 2023.
Shiibashi is the creator of the Illegal Rare , Tsukumogami: Teito Jōhōbu Tokkenchobōin Serosero Nanashiki , and Yui Kamio Lets Loose manga.

title: Rascal Does Not Dream Reveals Rio Futaba Memorial Visual
content:
Ahead of the Rascal Does Not Dream anime conclusion with the upcoming Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dear Friend movie, the series will release a memorial visual featuring the heroines of the series. First up is a memorial visual of Rio Futaba.

A video that features the real-life location in the visual was also released:

Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dear Friend anime movie is set to premiere on October 16 in Japan. The movie marks the anime’s conclusion. The voice cast includes:

• Asami Seto as Mai Sakurajima

• Kaito Ishikawa as Sakuta Azusagawa

• Reina Ueda as Mini-Skirt Santa

• Yurika Kubo as Kaede Azusagawa

• Nao Toyama as Tomoe Koga

• Atsumi Tanezaki as Rio Futaba

• Maaya Uchida as Nodoka Toyohama

• Inori Minase as Shoko Makinohara

• Sora Amamiya as Uzuki Hirokawa

• Aya Yamane as Ikumi Akagi

• Konomi Kohara as Sara Himeji

• Manaka Iwami as Miori ‌Mito

The Rascal Does Not Dream light novel series was written by Hajime Kamoshida, while Keji Mizoguchi drew the illustrations for it. ASCII Media Works began publishing it under their Dengeki Bunko imprint in April 2014, releasing a total of 15 volumes. The final volume was released on October 10, 2024, marking the end of the novel after 10 years of serialization.
author: Anime Corner

The final volume was released on October 10, 2024, marking the end of the novel after 10 years of serialization.

title: Straw Hats Are Off to Elbaph in One Piece Episode 1156 Preview Trailer
content:
ONE PIECE revealed the preview for Episode 1156, finally heading the Straw Hats to the long-awaited Elbaph. The anime is coming back after a 3-month break next week.

Episode 1156’s title is “The Long-sought Elbaph! The Big Reunion Banquet,” and you can watch the preview trailer below.

The ONE PIECE manga by Eiichiro Oda first began in July 1997, and the series exceeded 500 million circulating copies worldwide in August 2022. It also celebrated the 25th anniversary in 2022, while the anime adaptation started in October 1999 and is also still going strong. As Elbaph Arc got closer, hype was built with new cast additions . It culminated in Yuichi Nakamura getting cast as Loki , probably the most exciting character to make his debut in years and guaranteed to play a huge role in the endgame.

The anime will also be debuting new theme songs. The opening song’s title is “Luminous,” and it’s performed by AiNA THE END. The ending song will be “That Future” by Jisoku 36km.

Upcoming Projects

• Netflix’s live-action series has Season 3 already confirmed

• The franchise also has the upcoming remake anime by WIT Studio

• One Piece: Heroines novel focused on the female characters is getting an anime
author: Anime Corner

contentBlocks.text: ONE PIECE revealed the preview for Episode 1156, finally heading the Straw Hats to the long-awaited Elbaph. The anime is coming back after a 3-month break next week.
Episode 1156’s title is “The Long-sought Elbaph! The Big Reunion Banquet,” and you can watch the preview trailer below.

The ONE PIECE manga by Eiichiro Oda first began in July 1997, and the series exceeded 500 million circulating copies worldwide in August 2022. It also celebrated the 25th anniversary in 2022, while the anime adaptation started in October 1999 and is also still going strong. As Elbaph Arc got closer, hype was built with new cast additions . It culminated in Yuichi Nakamura getting cast as Loki , probably the most exciting character to make his debut in years and guaranteed to play a huge role in the endgame.
The anime will also be debuting new theme songs. The opening song’s title is “Luminous,” and it’s performed by AiNA THE END. The ending song will be “That Future” by Jisoku 36km.

title: Sound! Euphonium The Final Movie Reveals Stage Greeting Event, OST CD Covers
content:
Sound! Euphonium The Final Movie Part 1 stage greeting event has been revealed along with the CD cover jacket of the movie’s theme song “ToCoda” by TRUE and the original soundtrack “Melody across the Seasons.” Kyoto Animation is animating the movie, set to release on April 24.

AnimeJapan 2026 All Announcements and Highlights

The dates for the stage greeting events are as follows:

• April 24 (Fri) – Premiere Stage Greeting (at Marunouchi Piccadilly)

• April 25 (Sat) – Stage Greeting ~Tokyo Edition~ (at Shinjuku Piccadilly)

• April 26 (Sun) – Stage Greeting ~Kanagawa Edition~ (at Yokohama Burg 13/Kawasaki Cinecitta)

• May 2 (Sat) – Stage Greeting ~Nagoya Edition~ (at Midland Square Cinema/109 Cinemas Nagoya)

• May 3 (Sun) – Stage Greeting ~Osaka/Kyoto Edition~ (Namba Parks Cinema/MOVIX Kyoto)

• May 4 (Mon) – It’s Greenery Day Stage Greeting (Shinjuku Piccadilly)

Taichi Ogawa is directing and Tatsuya Ishihara is the chief director. Jukki Hanada is writing the script and series composition, and Shoko Ikeda and Kazumi Ikeda are handling the character designs. Akito Matsuda is composing the music. TRUE is singing the theme song“ToCoda.” The first season of the anime premiered in 2015, while the second season debuted in 2016, and the third season was broadcast in 2024, concluding in June of that year . There were also two movies between the first and second TV anime seasons— Sound Euphonium: Our Promise: A Brand New Day (2019) and Sound Euphonium: Ensemble Contest Arc (2023).

The cast includes:

• Tomoyo Kurosawa as Kumiko Oumae

• Chika Anzai as Reina Kosaka

• Ayaka Asai as Hazuki Kato

• Moe Toyota as Sapphire Kawashima

• Haruka Tomatsu as Mayu Kuroe

• Takahiro Sakurai as Noboru Taki

Crunchyroll is streaming all three seasons of the series, describing it as follows: Spring in the first year of high school. Kumiko, a member of the brass band in junior high school, visits the high school brass band club with classmates Hazuki and Sapphire. There, she comes across Reina, her former classmate from junior high. Hazuki and Sapphire decide to join the club, but Kumiko can’t make up her mind. She recollects her experience with Reina at a competition in junior high school.
author: Anime Corner

title: Tune In to the Midnight Heart Season 2 Reveals Teaser Visual, Trailer
content:
The previously announced Tune In to the Midnight Heart Season 2 revealed a teaser visual and trailer at AnimeJapan 2026. The release date of the sequel is yet to be announced.

Studio Gekko animated the first season and Masayuki Takahashi is the director. Yukie Sugawara wrote the script and Tomoyuki Shitaya designed the characters.

Suisei Hoshimachi is singing the opening theme song “Tsuki ni Mukatte Ute,” and Soala performs the ending theme song “Koe no Kiseki.” The main cast includes:

• Momoko Seto as Rikka Inohana

• Rumi Okubo as Nene Himekawa

• Sayumi Suzushiro as Kirino Iko

• Miku Ito as Shinobu Uzuki

• Rikuya Yasuda as Arisu Yamabuki

Tune In to the Midnight Heart anime is based on a manga series written by Masakuni Igarashi. It is available in English simultaneously through Kodansha’s K Manga digital platform. It describes the story: When Arisu Yamabuki was all alone in bed at night, he was able to find solace in the voice of a radio host who went by “Apollo.” However, one day, she simply stopped broadcasting without any explanation.Years then passed, and Arisu is now a second-year high-schooler. He makes it his mission to search for Apollo, as there is something he wants to tell her. He doesn’t know what she looks like, or even what her real name is, but he manages to get some leads on her in his school’s broadcasting club. That’s where he meets four girls who all dream to get a job where they can make full use of their voices!Just who is Apollo, and how will those four’s dreams pan out?
author: Anime Corner
